# Terraform GCP IP Reservation Module




GCP Module for reserving and managing static IP addresses (INTERNAL and EXTERNAL) with support for bulk allocation, IPv4/IPv6, and organizational tagging standards.


---

This project is part of our comprehensive approach towards DevOps Acceleration.

It's 100% Open Source and licensed under the [APACHE2](LICENSE).







We have [*lots of terraform modules*][terraform_modules] that are Open Source and we are trying to get them well-maintained!. Check them out!






## Introduction

This Terraform module simplifies the process of reserving and managing Google Cloud Platform (GCP) static IP addresses. It provides a reusable and configurable way to allocate IP addresses for various GCP resources such as compute instances, load balancers, and network interfaces. The module supports both INTERNAL (private) and EXTERNAL (public) IP addresses, IPv4 and IPv6 protocols, and bulk reservation capabilities. Built on Cloud Ops Works best practices, it ensures consistent labeling, organizational structure, and integration with existing GCP infrastructure.

## Usage


**IMPORTANT:** The `master` branch is used in `source` just as an example. In your code, do not pin to `master` because there may be breaking changes between releases.
Instead pin to the release tag (e.g. `?ref=vX.Y.Z`) of one of our [latest releases](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-gcp-ip-reservation/releases).


**IMPORTANT:** Do not pin to the `master` branch in your Terragrunt configuration. Instead, pin to a specific release tag (e.g., `?ref=v1.0.0`) to ensure stability and avoid breaking changes.

To use this module in a Terragrunt configuration, create a `terragrunt.hcl` file with the following structure:

```hcl
terraform {
  source = "github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-gcp-ip-reservation.git?ref=v1.0.0"
}

inputs = {
  reservation_number = 1   # (Optional) Number of reserved IP addresses. Default: 1.
  ip_settings = {          # (Optional) Map of settings for the IP address.
    address_type = "EXTERNAL"   # (Optional) Type: "EXTERNAL" or "INTERNAL". Default: "EXTERNAL".
    ip_version   = "IPV4"       # (Optional) Version: "IPV4" or "IPV6". Default: "IPV4".
    address      = null         # (Optional) Specific IP address. Default: null (auto-allocated).
    subnetwork   = null         # (Optional) Subnetwork for INTERNAL IPs. Default: null.
    description  = null         # (Optional) Description text. Default: null.
    region       = null         # (Optional) GCP region. Default: null (provider region).
  }
  is_hub = false   # (Optional) Hub/spoke configuration. Default: false.
  spoke_def = "001"   # (Optional) 3-digit spoke ID. Default: "001".
  org = {   # (Required) Organization details.
    organization_name = "example-org"   # (Required) Organization name.
    organization_unit = "operations"    # (Required) Organizational unit.
    environment_type  = "production"    # (Required) Environment type.
    environment_name  = "main"          # (Required) Environment name.
  }
  extra_tags = {   # (Optional) Additional labels. Default: {}.
    Owner = "CloudOps"
    Project = "MyProject"
  }
}
```

**Variables Documentation (YAML Format):**

```yaml
reservation_number: 1   # (Optional) Number of reserved IP addresses. Default: 1. Possible values: positive integers (1, 2, 3, ...).
ip_settings:   # (Optional) Map of settings for the IP address. Default: {}.
  address_type: "EXTERNAL"   # (Optional) Type of address. Default: "EXTERNAL". Possible values: "EXTERNAL", "INTERNAL".
  ip_version: "IPV4"         # (Optional) IP version. Default: "IPV4". Possible values: "IPV4", "IPV6".
  address: null              # (Optional) Specific static IP address. Default: null (auto-allocated). Format: valid IPv4/IPv6 string.
  subnetwork: null           # (Optional) Subnetwork for INTERNAL addresses. Default: null. Format: "projects/{ID}/regions/{REGION}/subnetworks/{NAME}".
  description: null          # (Optional) Description text. Default: null. Max 256 characters.
  region: null               # (Optional) GCP region. Default: null (provider region). Examples: "us-central1", "europe-west1".
is_hub: false   # (Optional) Hub/spoke configuration flag. Default: false. Possible values: true, false.
spoke_def: "001"   # (Optional) Spoke identifier. Default: "001". Must be 3-digit string (e.g., "001", "002", "100").
org:   # (Required) Organization metadata.
  organization_name: "example-org"   # (Required) Organization name. Any string.
  organization_unit: "operations"    # (Required) Unit/department name. Any string.
  environment_type: "production"     # (Required) Environment classification. Examples: "production", "staging", "development", "testing".
  environment_name: "main"           # (Required) Environment identifier. Any string.
extra_tags: {}   # (Optional) Additional labels to apply. Default: {}. Map of string keys to string values.
```

## Quick Start

Follow these steps to quickly reserve IP addresses using this module:

1. **Initialize Terragrunt**: Ensure you have Terragrunt installed and configured for GCP.

2. **Create Configuration File**: Create a `terragrunt.hcl` file in your project directory:

   ```hcl
   terraform {
     source = "github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-gcp-ip-reservation.git?ref=v1.0.0"
   }

   inputs = {
     org = {
       organization_name = "your-org"
       organization_unit = "your-unit"
       environment_type  = "production"
       environment_name  = "your-env"
     }
     reservation_number = 1
   }
   ```

3. **Authenticate with GCP**: Ensure you have proper GCP credentials:
   ```bash
   gcloud auth login
   gcloud config set project YOUR_PROJECT_ID
   ```

4. **Review the Plan**: Run Terragrunt plan to preview the IP reservation:
   ```bash
   terragrunt plan
   ```

5. **Apply the Configuration**: Execute the reservation:
   ```bash
   terragrunt apply
   ```

6. **Verify IPs**: Check the reserved IPs in GCP Console or via CLI:
   ```bash
   gcloud compute addresses list
   ```

The module will automatically generate IP names based on your organization structure (e.g., `eng-dev-prod-001-usc1-ip-1`) and apply consistent labels across all reserved addresses.


## Examples

### Example 1: Basic Single External IP Reservation

Reserve one external IPv4 address with default settings:

```hcl
terraform {
  source = "github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-gcp-ip-reservation.git?ref=v1.0.0"
}

inputs = {
  org = {
    organization_name = "acme-corp"
    organization_unit = "engineering"
    environment_type  = "staging"
    environment_name  = "dev"
  }
}
```

### Example 2: Bulk IP Reservation

Reserve 5 external IPv4 addresses for production:

```hcl
terraform {
  source = "github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-gcp-ip-reservation.git?ref=v1.0.0"
}

inputs = {
  reservation_number = 5
  org = {
    organization_name = "acme-corp"
    organization_unit = "platform"
    environment_type  = "production"
    environment_name  = "web"
  }
  extra_tags = {
    Application = "WebServer"
    Team = "Platform"
  }
}
```

### Example 3: Internal IP Reservation in Subnetwork

Reserve 2 internal IPv4 addresses within a specific subnetwork:

```hcl
terraform {
  source = "github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-gcp-ip-reservation.git?ref=v1.0.0"
}

inputs = {
  reservation_number = 2
  ip_settings = {
    address_type = "INTERNAL"
    ip_version   = "IPV4"
    subnetwork   = "projects/acme-corp/regions/us-central1/subnetworks/app-subnet"
    region       = "us-central1"
    description  = "Internal IPs for application tier"
  }
  org = {
    organization_name = "acme-corp"
    organization_unit = "applications"
    environment_type  = "production"
    environment_name  = "app-tier"
  }
}
```

### Example 4: IPv6 External IP Reservation

Reserve external IPv6 addresses:

```hcl
terraform {
  source = "github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-gcp-ip-reservation.git?ref=v1.0.0"
}

inputs = {
  ip_settings = {
    address_type = "EXTERNAL"
    ip_version   = "IPV6"
  }
  org = {
    organization_name = "acme-corp"
    organization_unit = "network"
    environment_type  = "production"
    environment_name  = "ipv6-services"
  }
}
```

### Example 5: Specific IP Address Reservation

Reserve a specific static IP address (if available in the pool):

```hcl
terraform {
  source = "github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-gcp-ip-reservation.git?ref=v1.0.0"
}

inputs = {
  ip_settings = {
    address_type = "EXTERNAL"
    ip_version   = "IPV4"
    address      = "35.192.45.100"
    description  = "Reserved IP for load balancer"
  }
  org = {
    organization_name = "acme-corp"
    organization_unit = "infrastructure"
    environment_type  = "production"
    environment_name  = "lb"
  }
}
```

### Example 6: Hub-Spoke Configuration with Multiple IPs

Hub configuration with 3 IPs for a spoke network:

```hcl
terraform {
  source = "github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-gcp-ip-reservation.git?ref=v1.0.0"
}

inputs = {
  reservation_number = 3
  is_hub = true
  spoke_def = "001"
  ip_settings = {
    address_type = "INTERNAL"
    ip_version   = "IPV4"
    subnetwork   = "projects/acme-corp/regions/europe-west1/subnetworks/spoke-001"
    region       = "europe-west1"
  }
  org = {
    organization_name = "acme-corp"
    organization_unit = "network-ops"
    environment_type  = "production"
    environment_name  = "europe"
  }
  extra_tags = {
    NetworkType = "Spoke"
    Region = "EU"
  }
}
```

## Inputs

| Name | Description | Type | Default | Required |
|------|-------------|------|---------|:--------:|
| <a name="input_extra_tags"></a> [extra\_tags](#input\_extra\_tags) | Extra tags to add to the resources | `map(string)` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_ip_settings"></a> [ip\_settings](#input\_ip\_settings) | (optional) Map of settings for the IP address | <pre>object({<br/>    address_type = optional(string, "EXTERNAL")<br/>    ip_version   = optional(string, "IPV4")<br/>    address      = optional(string, null)<br/>    subnetwork   = optional(string, null)<br/>    description  = optional(string, null)<br/>    region       = optional(string, null)<br/>  })</pre> | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_is_hub"></a> [is\_hub](#input\_is\_hub) | Is this a hub or spoke configuration? | `bool` | `false` | no |
| <a name="input_org"></a> [org](#input\_org) | Organization details | <pre>object({<br/>    organization_name = string<br/>    organization_unit = string<br/>    environment_type  = string<br/>    environment_name  = string<br/>  })</pre> | n/a | yes |
| <a name="input_reservation_number"></a> [reservation\_number](#input\_reservation\_number) | (optional) Number of reserved IP addresses, defaults to 1 | `number` | `1` | no |
| <a name="input_spoke_def"></a> [spoke\_def](#input\_spoke\_def) | Spoke ID Number, must be a 3 digit number | `string` | `"001"` | no |

## Outputs

| Name | Description |
|------|-------------|
| <a name="output_address_type"></a> [address\_type](#output\_address\_type) | Type of addresses (INTERNAL/EXTERNAL) |
| <a name="output_ip_version"></a> [ip\_version](#output\_ip\_version) | IP version of reserved addresses |
| <a name="output_public_ip_ids"></a> [public\_ip\_ids](#output\_public\_ip\_ids) | Map of reserved IP addresses (name => id) |
| <a name="output_public_ips"></a> [public\_ips](#output\_public\_ips) | List of reserved IP addresses |
| <a name="output_region"></a> [region](#output\_region) | Region where IPs were reserved |
| <a name="output_reservation_number"></a> [reservation\_number](#output\_reservation\_number) | Number of reserved IP addresses |
